title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Between life and death there is a library, and within that library, the shelves go on forever. Every book provides a chance to try another life you could have lived.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: An Easy & Proven Way to Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones. No matter your goals, Atomic Habits offers a proven framework for improving every day.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, Dune is the story of Paul Atreides, heir to a noble family tasked with ruling an inhospitable world where the only item of value is spice.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A Handbook of Agile Software Craftsmanship. Even bad code can function. But if code isn't clean, it can bring a development organization to its knees.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: Alicia Berenson's life is seemingly perfect. A famous painter married to an in-demand fashion photographer, she lives in a grand house. Then one evening, she shoots her husband five times in the face.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: Explore how Homo sapiens conquered the earth, through a masterful blend of science and history that challenges everything we knew about being human.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: Ryland Grace is the sole survivor on a desperate, last-chance mission to save humanity from extinction—and right now, he doesn't even remember his own name.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: The Big Ideas Behind Reliable, Scalable, and Maintainable Systems. Key concepts for data architecture, distributed processing, and modern databases.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it, exploring courage and compassion.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: The famous tour of the mind explaining the two systems that drive the way we think: System 1 is fast and intuitive; System 2 is slow and logical.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
description: Timeless lessons on wealth, greed, and happiness. Doing well with money isn't necessarily about what you know. It's about how you behave.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A glorious fantasy classic following Bilbo Baggins as he is whisked away on an unexpected journey to reclaim the lost Hobbit Kingdom of Erebor.
